summary: Large matching pass for src/melee/it/items/itlikelike.c. The PR replaced many placeholder stubs with C implementations for Like Like item state, motion, collision, damage, dropped/thrown, capture/release, and spawn logic. It also expanded Like Like attributes and item-vars layouts in itCommonItems.h, tightened itlikelike.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures, and updated ftCo_800C7B0C's fighter-side prototype so Like Like release/collision code could call it with typed arguments. decomp.dev reported +7956 matched bytes, 42 new matches, and 6 remaining near-matches/improvements; the author's ready-for-review comment listed only four functions still below the 99.999% threshold.

key_files.role: Main Like Like item decompilation; added 1083 lines and replaced many placeholder markers with matched or near-matched implementations.
Updated Like Like prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed Item_GObj/HSD_GObj/Vec3 signatures and added externs.
Expanded itLikelikeAttributes and itLikelike_ItemVars layouts with offset comments, Vec3 fields, HurtCapsule pointer, timers/state fields, and padding.
Changed ftCo_800C7B0C definition signature from unknown params to typed fighter/collision arguments while leaving the body NOT_IMPLEMENTED.
Changed ftCo_800C7B0C declaration to match the typed signature needed by Like Like release logic.

what_changed: Implemented broad portions of Like Like item behavior, including detection of nearby fighters via ftLib_8008627C/ftLib_80086644, movement state transitions, collision handlers, damage-received behavior, dropped behavior, grab/capture release behavior, and spawn helper it_802DC4BC.

Added item state helpers such as it_802D9A2C, it_802D9BA8, it_802DA104, it_802DA4C0, it_802DA8D8, it_802DA960, it_802DAA10, it_802DBAF0, it_802DB74C, it_802DB8A8, it_802DB9F4, it_802DBA68, it_802DC0AC, and it_802DC4BC.
Implemented many Like Like motion callbacks, including UnkMotion0/1/2/5/6/7/10/13/14/15/17/18-related animation, physics, and collision functions where previously placeholders remained.
Introduced a local static inline helper itSwapVelocity(Item* ip) to centralize facing_dir inversion and x40_vel.x recalculation using GET_ATTRS(ip)->x0->y.
Expanded itLikelikeAttributes from a minimal two-field placeholder into a typed offset-based layout with Vec3* x0, integer timer/threshold fields, float ranges, and s8 fields x3C-x3E.
Expanded itLikelike_ItemVars with Vec3 x20, x40/x44/x48 integer fields, and HurtCapsule* x54, while preserving offset comments tied to item variable offsets such as ip+DF4 and ip+E28.

Replaced several unknown prototypes in itlikelike.h with concrete return and argument types, including bool it_802D9A2C(Item_GObj*), void it_802DAD18(HSD_GObj*), void helper transitions, and Item_GObj* it_802DC4BC(s32, Vec3*).
Added necessary includes for math, camera, ft_0C31, ftlib, item collision/common items, lb helpers/types, mp collision, and baselib random.
Updated ftCo_800C7B0C to accept Fighter_GObj*, two void* vector-like arguments, lbColl_80008D30_arg1*, and f32, enabling typed calls from it_802DBAF0.
smart_moves: The PR used structure-layout discovery to unlock matching: the Like Like attributes and item-vars became explicit enough for code to reference Vec3 positions, timers, hurt capsule storage, state flags, and thresholds instead of opaque padding.

The local itSwapVelocity helper reduced duplicated direction-flip code and documented that the speed source is attr->x0->y after the attribute struct was corrected from the old x0->x4 placeholder access.
The fighter-side ftCo_800C7B0C prototype was improved just enough for the item code to call it with meaningful typed arguments without needing to implement the fighter function itself.
The author explicitly tracked remaining near-matches with tools/easy_funcs.py and reported exact addresses, function names, sizes, and match percentages before review.
The code kept offset comments in headers, which makes future item variable/attribute work easier to audit against disassembly offsets.
Near-match comments in the code called out specific register-swap problems for it_802DAD18 and it_802DC4BC instead of hiding the remaining matching issue.
decomp_lessons: For item decompilation, improving article special-attributes and xDD4 item-var structs can be a prerequisite for matching behavior code cleanly.
When an item interacts with fighter capture/release code, it may be better to type the cross-module fighter prototype than to keep UNK_PARAMS and force awkward item-side code.

Keep unknown field names when semantics are not proven, but add accurate offsets, widths, and concrete primitive/container types; this PR used xNN names while replacing large pads with Vec3, s32, s8, and HurtCapsule* fields.

A small local inline can be acceptable when a shared inline cannot work because it depends on a file-local macro or dynamic attribute accessor; the PR explicitly notes that itSwapVelocity could not go in inlines.h because of dynamic GET_ATTRS.
Document near-match blockers in code or PR comments with precise causes like register swaps and exact easy_funcs percentages so future contributors know where to resume.

Be cautious with attribute substructure uncertainty: the code used pointer arithmetic casts in it_802DBAF0 and left a TODO for pointer hacks, which is useful evidence that part of the layout was not fully understood.
naming_conventions: Preserved project address-style unknown names such as it_802D9A2C, it_802DBAF0, and it_802DC4BC when semantic names were not established.
Used item state callback naming pattern itLikelike_UnkMotionN_Anim, itLikelike_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itLikelike_UnkMotionN_Coll.
Used logic callback naming pattern it_2725_Logic5_DmgReceived, it_2725_Logic5_Dropped, itLikeLike_Logic5_PickedUp, itLikeLike_Logic5_Thrown, and itLikeLike_Logic5_Destroyed.
Used offset-based unknown field names in structs, such as x18, x1C, x3C, x44, and x54, with comments documenting exact offsets.
Added/kept extern naming with address comments, including it_803F8468 and it_804D6D40.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK with varying sizes, including PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(24), PAD_STACK(0x20), and PAD_STACK(0x16), to shape stack layout for matching.
Used UNUSED local byte arrays and temporary locals to influence stack/register allocation in functions such as ECB manipulation and near-matching functions.
Used gotos and block labels in complex physics/collision functions to mirror control flow and preserve matching behavior.
Used explicit casts to callback signatures, such as void (*)(HSD_GObj*) and HSD_GObj* casts in Item_80268E5C calls, to satisfy existing API types and compiler output.
Used ABS and manual absolute-value forms depending on the function, suggesting expression-shape sensitivity for matching.
Used HSD_Randi/HSD_Randf, M_PI, and HSD_JObjSetRotationZ in ways matching item state transitions and orientation behavior.
Commented that it_802DAD18 was one r30-vs-r31 register swap away from matching and it_802DC4BC was one r29-r30 register swap away from being solved.
Used pointer arithmetic casts like (lbColl_80008D30_arg1*)((u8*)attr + 0x64) and +0x40 in it_802DBAF0 with a TODO to fix pointer hacks, indicating a temporary matching/layout tactic.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code increase to 53.70%, +0.20%, +7956 bytes, with 42 new matches and 6 improvements in unmatched items for main/melee/it/items/itlikelike.

Marked the PR ready for review and listed only four remaining functions below the easy_funcs threshold: it_802DAD18 at 99.10%, it_2725_Logic5_DmgReceived at 98.26%, itLikelike_UnkMotion1_Coll at 99.84%, and it_802DC4BC at 99.12%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching it_802DAD18, it_2725_Logic5_DmgReceived, itLikelike_UnkMotion1_Coll, or it_802DC4BC.
Search for later cleanup of the TODO pointer hacks in it_802DBAF0 and the attr + 0x40/+0x64 lbColl_80008D30_arg1 casts.
Search for later renames or semantic typing of itLikelikeAttributes fields x18, x1C, x20, x24, x28, x2C, x30, x34, x38, x3C, x3D, and x3E.
Search for later struct cleanup of itLikelike_ItemVars x40/x44/x48, which this PR comments are likely some form of S32Vec.
Search for PRs touching ftCo_800C7B0C after this typed prototype change.
